notes.title: 10.000 steps
notes.text:
eight kilometres
What does 10,000 steps look like? Ten thousand steps equates to about eight kilometres, or an hour and 40 minutes walking, depending on your stride length and walking speed.
so 5000 steps is equal to 4 km and 50 mins of walking ..

I usually do 4 km in 40 min and that equals 5800 steps ... 
which means that i do 2 km in 20 min and 2900 or 3000 steps 
if I walk fast for an hr I could do about 9000 steps 
if I follow it up with another 20 min walk in the evening then I am done for the day ...

notes.title: 20 things to do before 8 a.m. to start living a better life
notes.text:
1)Wake up early
2)Get up right away
3)Make your bed
4)Stretch
5)Drink water
6)Meditate
7)Breathe deeply
8)Write in a journal
9)Exercise
10)Get some sunlight
11)Drink coffee at home
12)Eat a healthy breakfast
13)Spend time with loved ones
14)Read
15)Pack your lunch
16)Disconnect
17)Set goals for the day
18)Practice gratitude
19)Work on a personal passion project

annotations.title: Ayesha Sanghi on Instagram: "Electric hot & cold gua sha #notsponsored Comment for the link! Stumbled across this ‘smart’ gua sha on Amazon and had to try it! I cannot tell you how GOOD it is and how well it works too. It has a really effective metal edge for sculpting and can be used as is or in 1 of its 3 modes. Red: Warms the metal edge and vibrates to help prevent fine lines and improves product absorption, this will be extra nice in winters Blue: This is my favourite! It turns the metal edge literally freezing cold in under 30 seconds, the fastest cooling I’ve ever experienced and it’s amazing for depuffing and for calming cystic acne Purple: This has micro currents for tightening, toning and lifting. This one can be intimidating to use because it feels like little electric shocks so ease into it I always apply a facial oil or serum first to make sure the gua sha glides easily. This is from a brand called @numour_official and they have a few more products which I’m testing too!"
annotations.description:
48K likes, 4,323 comments - globalbeautyfinds on October 12, 2024: "Electric hot & cold gua sha 🤯 #notsponsored 
Comment for the link!

Stumbled across this ‘smart’ gua sha on Amazon and had to try it! I cannot tell you how GOOD it is and how well it works too. It has a really effective metal edge for sculpting and can be used as is or in 1 of its 3 modes. 

Red: Warms the metal edge and vibrates to help prevent fine lines and improves product absorption, this will be extra nice in winters
Blue: This is my favourite! It turns the metal edge literally freezing cold in under 30 seconds, the fastest cooling I’ve ever experienced and it’s amazing for depuffing and for calming cystic acne
Purple: This has micro currents for tightening, toning and lifting. This one can be intimidating to use because it feels like little electric shocks so ease into it

I always apply a facial oil or serum first to make sure the gua sha glides easily. 

This is from a brand called @numour_official and they have a few more products which I’m testing too!".

Restaurant bar in mumbai

annotations.title: ELLE India on Instagram: "#ELLELifestyle: The team behind Ekaa and KMC* is throwing the spotlight on Daaru and Chakhna with this new bar in Bandra. Bombay Daak (@bombay.daak) by Chef Niyati Rao and Sagar Neve borrows inspiration from dive bars across India and brings you fun iterations of daaru and chakhna from family recipes, conversations with friends and family, and stories contributed by people from India. The name Bombay Daak carries a historical resonance. In pre-colonial times, the British developed a fondness for Bombay Duck or ‘Bombil,’ as an accompaniment to alcohol. This fish was transported to Calcutta (Kolkata) via a train service known as Bombay Daak—where ‘Daak’ means ‘mail’ in Hindi. This narrative of cultural exchange mirrors Bombay Daak’s ethos. “The name for us means something more. It’s a metaphor of us going all around the country and coming back. It represents the metaphor that we went to each and every place in India, connected to and with each other and then came back to the HQ where it all started,” chef Rao shares. The bar’s logo is derived from an old Indian folk tale of a feline with a fish in its jaw. It depicts a presumably intoxicated tiger with a bombil in its mouth, symbolises indulgence, pleasure and the joy of experiencing food and drink without societal taboos. Much like what you’ll experience at Bombay Daak. Tune in to get a glimpse of this experience. . Words and video by: @eatstylego . #NewRestaurant #Bandra #MumbaiRestaurants #Bars #BombayDaak (Mumbai, Bandra Bars, Bombay Daak, New Restaurant)"
annotations.description:

The name Bombay Daak carries a historical resonance. In pre-colonial times, the British developed a fondness for Bombay Duck or ‘Bombil,’ as an accompaniment to alcohol. This fish was transported to Calcutta (Kolkata) via a train service known as Bombay Daak—where ‘Daak’ means ‘mail’ in Hindi. This narrative of cultural exchange mirrors Bombay Daak’s ethos. 

“The name for us means something more. It’s a metaphor of us going all around the country and coming back. It represents the metaphor that we went to each and every place in India, connected to and with each other and then came back to the HQ where it all started,” chef Rao shares. 

The bar’s logo is derived from an old Indian folk tale of a feline with a fish in its jaw. It depicts a presumably intoxicated tiger with a bombil in its mouth, symbolises indulgence, pleasure and the joy of experiencing food and drink without societal taboos. Much like what you’ll experience at Bombay Daak.

Tune in to get a glimpse of this experience.

Cacao is the core ingredient in chocolate and is produced from the beans of cacao tree through a process of fermentation, drying and roasting. Clinical studies have shown that men in their sixties with heart disease, who drank 2 cups of dark hot cocoa per day for a month, doubled their stem cells and significantly improved their circulation. annotations.description:
wellnessesteps on October 1, 2024: "Cacao: The Superfood of Superfoods 🍫

World-renowned physician and scientist Dr. William Li explains how cacao can stimulate stem cells to migrate to various sites of repair and regeneration throughout the body. 

Cacao is the core ingredient in chocolate and is produced from the beans of cacao tree through a process of fermentation, drying and roasting. 

Clinical studies have shown that men in their sixties with heart disease, who drank 2 cups of dark hot cocoa per day for a month, doubled their stem cells and significantly improved their circulation.

The COSMOS (COcoa Supplement and Multivitamin Outcomes Study) is a large scale clinical trial that recently showed that consuming high flavanol cocoa decreases the risk of cardiovascular death.

▪️Effect of cocoa flavanol supplementation for the prevention of cardiovascular disease events: the COcoa Supplement and Multivitamin Outcomes Study (COSMOS) randomized clinical trial:

https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0002916522002751

▪️Effect of Cocoa Beverage and Dark Chocolate Consumption on Blood Pressure in Those with Normal and Elevated Blood Pressure: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis:

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9265772/

▪️Improvement of endothelial function with dietary flavanols is associated with mobilization of circulating angiogenic cells in patients with coronary artery disease:

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20620742/

▪️Effects of cocoa products intake on cardiometabolic biomarkers of type 2 diabetes patients: a systematic review and meta-analysis based on both long-term and short-term randomised controlled trials:

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35253583/

What are your thoughts on this?

Credits ~
Speaker: @drwilliamli
Video: @drmarkhyman 

Disclaimer:  We are not offering health advice on this page.  Content is for informational purposes only.  Please consult with medical professional before applying any material contained.".

notes.title: Untitled note
notes.text:
Adionics is a private French company created in 2012 after five years of research with the ambition of becoming a leading technology provider in the desalination supply chain. Thanks to the patented technology, AquaOmnes®, Adionics can tackle the needs of a wide variety of industries and cities, including those whose demands are currently unmet. Adionics’ team is made of PhDs and engineers from top-tier universities with expertise in chemicals, oil and gas, energy conversion and the water industry.

What do we do?

After five years of research, Adionics created a revolutionary family of solvents that is able to extract salts from sea water or brine. While other desalination solutions extract water from the salt, Adionics does the contrary, which allows the process to be more efficient, sustainable and customizable.

notes.text: International driving licence https://www.instagram.com/reel/C-IdLmRIj66/?igsh=MWpxZnBkNTU5bTJlMQ==

annotations.title: Tanya Khanijow on Instagram: "Easiest way of getting the international Driver’s permit for 3 years! If you read any post about IDP online, it says that the process is very easy. But in my experience, I faced a lot of issues in applying for it online so I had to visit the RTO. ‼️ And you can’t visit the nearest RTO for the same. It has to be the RTO of the city where your drivers license was issued. ‼️Also, among the documents needed, you have to submit 3 ids with matching addresses and depending on your state, even valid visa, and return flight tickets. ‼️You’ll receive the IDP valid for 1 year, for only 1 country. You may also apply for the same at https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan//en PRO: If you apply through the RTO, the fees is more economical at ₹1000-1500 But if you want to finish the process in 10 minutes, with zero hassles and only basic documents like your licence needed, visit www.internationaldriversassociation.com The fees is higher at approximately ₹6500 for a 3 year visa, but it is valid for 150 countries! So easy and convenient! SAVE THIS FOR YOUR TRAVELS! P.S. music remixed is by @avneet.music Was this helpful!"
annotations.description:
90K likes, 687 comments - tanyakhanijow on August 1, 2024: "Easiest way of getting the international Driver’s permit for 3 years! 

If you read any post about IDP online, it says that the process is very easy. But in my experience, I faced a lot of issues in applying for it online so I had to visit the RTO. 

‼️ And you can’t visit the nearest RTO for the same. It has to be the RTO of the city where your drivers license was issued. 
‼️Also, among the documents needed, you have to submit 3 ids with matching addresses and depending on your state, even valid visa, and return flight tickets. 
‼️You’ll receive the IDP valid for 1 year, for only 1 country. 

You may also apply for the same at https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan//en 

PRO: If you apply through the RTO, the fees is more economical at ₹1000-1500 

But if you want to finish the process in 10 minutes, with zero hassles and only basic documents like your licence needed, visit  www.internationaldriversassociation.com 

The fees is higher at approximately ₹6500 for a 3 year visa, but it is valid for 150 countries! So easy and convenient! 

SAVE THIS FOR YOUR TRAVELS! 

P.S. music remixed is by @avneet.music 

Was this helpful!".

international driving licence https://www.instagram.com/reel/c-idlmrij66/?igsh=mwpxznbkntu5btjlmq== tanya khanijow on instagram: "easiest way of getting the international driver’s permit for 3 years! if you read any post about idp online, it says that the process is very easy. but in my experience, i faced a lot of issues in applying for it online so i had to visit the rto. ‼️ and you can’t visit the nearest rto for the same. it has to be the rto of the city where your drivers license was issued. ‼️also, among the documents needed, you have to submit 3 ids with matching addresses and depending on your state, even valid visa, and return flight tickets. ‼️you’ll receive the idp valid for 1 year, for only 1 country. you may also apply for the same at https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan//en pro: if you apply through the rto, the fees is more economical at ₹1000-1500 but if you want to finish the process in 10 minutes, with zero hassles and only basic documents like your licence needed, visit www.internationaldriversassociation.com the fees is higher at approximately ₹6500 for a 3 year visa, but it is valid for 150 countries! so easy and convenient! save this for your travels! p.s. music remixed is by @avneet.music was this helpful!" 90k likes, 687 comments - tanyakhanijow on august 1, 2024: "easiest way of getting the international driver’s permit for 3 years! 

if you read any post about idp online, it says that the process is very easy. but in my experience, i faced a lot of issues in applying for it online so i had to visit the rto. 

‼️ and you can’t visit the nearest rto for the same. it has to be the rto of the city where your drivers license was issued. 
‼️also, among the documents needed, you have to submit 3 ids with matching addresses and depending on your state, even valid visa, and return flight tickets. 
‼️you’ll receive the idp valid for 1 year, for only 1 country. 

you may also apply for the same at https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan//en 

pro: if you apply through the rto, the fees is more economical at ₹1000-1500 

but if you want to finish the process in 10 minutes, with zero hassles and only basic documents like your licence needed, visit  www.internationaldriversassociation.com 

the fees is higher at approximately ₹6500 for a 3 year visa, but it is valid for 150 countries! so easy and convenient! 

save this for your travels! 

p.s. music remixed is by @avneet.music 

was this helpful!". https://www.instagram.com/tanyakhanijow/reel/c-idlmrij66/

annotations.title: Isa Khan | Traveller on Instagram: "6-day Bali itinerary: Day 1: We will land at the airport and head to Sidemen to check in at the hotel, then rest for the day. Day 2: We will cover 2 places. First, we will go to Tirta Gangga, which is a perfect Instagrammable spot, and then we will go to Lahangan Sweet to watch a beautiful sunset. Day 3: You will explore Ubud’s Monkey Forest and the famous Tegallalang rice fields. Day 4: Visit the dreamy Sari Timbul, and then we will head to Beach Love to see a beautiful sunset. Day 5: You will spend time at another unique location called Tanah Barak, with its fascinating limestone walls on the beach. Day 6: Just relax at Soan Galuh Beach where you will see the most beautiful sunset of your life. Pro tip: It would be best if you hire a scooter to travel to all these places, as it would be the most economical way. Also, try to start your day early so that you don’t have to rush from one place to another. Some of these places get crowded later in the day, so it’s better to reach them early. Happy traveling, stay safe, and make sure to avoid using plastic and leaving any waste at these beautiful spots. . . . #bali #mustvisit #travelreels #travelbali"

Day 5: You will spend time at another unique location called Tanah Barak, with its fascinating limestone walls on the beach.

Day 6: Just relax at Soan Galuh Beach where you will see the most beautiful sunset of your life.

Happy traveling, stay safe, and make sure to avoid using plastic and leaving any waste at these beautiful spots.

annotations.title: Sourabh Godbole on Instagram: "Steal Like an Artist! Are you ready to ignite your creativity and transform your ideas into extraordinary art? Austin Kleon’s “Steal Like an Artist” is the ultimate guide to embracing influence, discovering your unique voice, and finding inspiration in everyday life. This book teaches you: How to “steal” from your inspirations and turn them into something uniquely yours. ️ The importance of starting now and learning by doing. ✍️ Why creating for yourself leads to authentic, impactful work. ❤️ The benefits of using your hands and balancing digital with physical creativity. ️ How side projects and hobbies can fuel your main creative pursuits. ️ Remember, perfection is the enemy of progress! Focus on producing good work consistently, and greatness will follow. Join a global community of like-minded creatives and find your tribe online. Be kind, network, and build positive relationships that open doors to new opportunities. Routine and discipline are your allies in maintaining long-term creativity. Establish daily habits and embrace limitations as they foster innovation. Ready to take your creativity to the next level? Dive into “Steal Like an Artist” and start your journey toward creative brilliance today! ✨ #CreativeGenius #StealLikeAnArtist #unlockyourpotential #CreativeJourney #BookLovers #instagood #books #bookstagram #book #booklover #reading #bookworm #bookstagrammer #read #booknerd #bookaddict #booksofinstagram #bibliophile #instabook #love #bookshelf #readersofinstagram #booksbooksbooks #bookaholic #reader #bookphotography#instabooks #booklovers"
annotations.description:
55K likes, 307 comments - sourabh.godbole on May 26, 2024: "Steal Like an Artist! 📚💡

Are you ready to ignite your creativity and transform your ideas into extraordinary art? Austin Kleon’s “Steal Like an Artist” is the ultimate guide to embracing influence, discovering your unique voice, and finding inspiration in everyday life. 🌟🚀

This book teaches you:

How to “steal” from your inspirations and turn them into something uniquely yours. 🖼️🔍

The importance of starting now and learning by doing. 📈✍️

Why creating for yourself leads to authentic, impactful work. ❤️📖

The benefits of using your hands and balancing digital with physical creativity. 🖌️💻

How side projects and hobbies can fuel your main creative pursuits. 🎸🏞️

Remember, perfection is the enemy of progress! Focus on producing good work consistently, and greatness will follow. 🌱🏆

Join a global community of like-minded creatives and find your tribe online. 🌐🤝 

Be kind, network, and build positive relationships that open doors to new opportunities. 🌍🌈

Credit card

annotations.title: Prakriti Arora | Ashish ✈️ Travel Couple on Instagram: "Read the detailed guide below ⤵️ This is an ultra premium credit card and also one of the best cards for travel. This is Axis Magnus Burgundy. Lets talk about the reward benefits on this card ⤵️ ❤️Earn 12 Reward points per Rs. 200 on spends up to Rs. 1.50 Lakhs and 35 Reward points per Rs. 200 on incremental spends above Rs. 1.50 lakhs in a calendar month. ❤️Earn 4 miles for every 5 Reward points across various airlines and hotel. ❤️Earn 5X reward on bookings made via Axis TravelEdge portal. ❤️Unlimited lounge access globally via Priority Pass ❤️Complimentary airport meet and assist services ❤️Rent payments are also eligible for reward points till Rs.50,000/month ♥️ Low forex markup of 2% ❤️Other than travel, you can also get Rs.1,000 off on food bill of Rs.2,500 via Eazydiner How did we earn 9 lakh points? ➡️We got the advantage of last year’s 25K bonus miles offer for 4 months. ➡️We were setting up our home, going on international trips, and shopping for our wedding. We got overall 3x rewards on spends above Rs. 1.5 lakhs/month. (reward rate is 4.8% below 1.5L and 14% above 1.5L ) ➡️Heavily using Axis Travel portal for booking flights. (reward rate is 24%) ➡️Puchasing gift vouchers for everything. From Swiggy to Coach, we used gift cards everywhere.(reward rate is between 10% - 24%) ➡️Paying our monthly rent via credit card (reward rate is 3% but this helps in reaching 1.5L monthly milestone) How do we book international business class flights? ➡️Axis has the maximum travel partners when it comes to converting your reward points into miles and we generally convert our points to Singapore Airlines or United Airlines. ➡️Business/First class flights generally cost 40K points from India to any SEA country and 60K points from India to Europe. How did we get this card? ✅ We registered ourselves as Axis Burgundy customer during the last quarter of 2023 and upgraded our Magnus to Magnus Burgundy LTF in Jan ‘24 Other equally good cards are HDFC Infinia and Axis Atlas #travelhacks #businessclassflyer #creditcardpoints #travelcredit"
annotations.description:
9,973 likes, 135 comments - escapetolandscapes on May 8, 2024: "Read the detailed guide below ⤵️

This is an ultra premium credit card and also one of the best cards for travel. This is Axis Magnus Burgundy.

Lets talk about the reward benefits on this card ⤵️
❤️Earn 12 Reward points per Rs. 200 on spends up to Rs. 1.50 Lakhs and 35 Reward points per Rs. 200 on incremental spends above Rs. 1.50 lakhs in a calendar month.
❤️Earn 4 miles for every 5 Reward points across various airlines and hotel.
❤️Earn 5X reward on bookings made via Axis TravelEdge portal.
❤️Unlimited lounge access globally via Priority Pass
❤️Complimentary airport meet and assist services
❤️Rent payments are also eligible for reward points till Rs.50,000/month
♥️ Low forex markup of 2%
❤️Other than travel, you can also get Rs.1,000 off on food bill of Rs.2,500 via Eazydiner 

How did we earn 9 lakh points?
➡️We got the advantage of last year’s 25K bonus miles offer for 4 months. 
➡️We were setting up our home, going on international trips, and shopping for our wedding. We got overall 3x rewards on spends above Rs. 1.5 lakhs/month. (reward rate is 4.8% below 1.5L and 14% above 1.5L )
➡️Heavily using Axis Travel portal for booking flights. (reward rate is 24%)
➡️Puchasing gift vouchers for everything. From Swiggy to Coach, we used gift cards everywhere.(reward rate is between 10% - 24%) 
➡️Paying our monthly rent via credit card (reward rate is 3% but this helps in reaching 1.5L monthly milestone) 

How do we book international business class flights?
➡️Axis has the maximum travel partners when it comes to converting your reward points into miles and we generally convert our points to Singapore Airlines or United Airlines. 
➡️Business/First class flights generally cost 40K points from India to any SEA country and 60K points from India to Europe. 

How did we get this card?
✅ We registered ourselves as Axis Burgundy customer during the last quarter of 2023 and upgraded our Magnus to Magnus Burgundy LTF in Jan ‘24

💎Other equally good cards are HDFC Infinia and Axis Atlas

#travelhacks #businessclassflyer #creditcardpoints #travelcredit".

notes.text:
Heart health stairs 

Hospital A Coruña, Spain. "If it takes you more than one and a half minutes to ascend four flights of stairs, your health is suboptimal, and it would be a good idea to consult a doctor."


 ketosis?
The most reliable and accurate method of measuring ketosis is to measure your blood ketone levels using a specialized meter. This is the method is used in most research studies (9). The meter measures your ketone levels by calculating the amount of beta-hydroxybutyrate (BHB) in your blood.
According to some experts on the keto diet, nutritional ketosis is defined as blood ketones equal to or above 0.5 millimolar (mM) (10).

annotations.title: Riya | Indian in Germany on Instagram: "Save this reel and apply ✈️ Follow @riyabangia_ I help to navigate you to study, move and work abroad. ✈️ Here is a list of some of the countries that currently offer a digital nomad visa: * Dominica * Mexico * Anguilla * Costa Rica * Dubai * Antigua and Barbuda * Ecuador * Aruba * Estonia * North Macedonia * Brazil * Bermuda * Georgia * Montserrat * Norway * Bahamas * Germany * Panama * Portugal * Barbados * Greece * Romania * Belize * Hungary * Saint Lucia * Cayman Islands * Colombia * Iceland * Seychel What Is a Digital Nomad Visa? Digital nomad visas are permits that allow you to work remotely in a foreign country. You usually have to work for an employer or company outside of your host country or have a business you can manage from anywhere in the world to qualify for this visa. Eligibility Criteria For a Digital Nomad Visa Here are some eligibility criteria you have to meet for the digital nomad visa: * You must be over 18 years of age. * You must have a specific monthly income (the amount varies from country to country). * You must have a job you can do from anywhere in the world. notes.text:
પર્યાવરણના પ્રશ્નો મુદ્દે ચર્ચા:અંકલેશ્વર અને પાનોલી ઇન્ડસ્ટ્રીઝ એસોસિએશનના સભ્યોએ પર્યાવરણના પ્રશ્નોને લઈને ગાંધીનગરના મંત્રીઓ સાથે બેઠક યોજી

અંકલેશ્વર6 કલાક પહેલા

અંકલેશ્વર તેમજ પાનોલી ઇન્ડસ્ટ્રીઝ એસોસિએશનના પ્રતિનિધિઓએ પર્યાવરણના પ્રશ્નોને લઈને ગઈકાલે ગુરૂવારના રોજ ગાંધીનગરમાં વન-પર્યાવરણ મંત્રી કિરીટસિંહ રાણાની અધ્યક્ષતામાં તેમની સાથે ઉદ્યોગમંત્રી જગદીશ પંચાલની ઉપસ્થિતમાં એસોસિએશનના પ્રતિનિધિઓ સાથે એક ઇન્ટરેક્ટિવ રાઉન્ડ ટેબલ મીટિંગ યોજાઈ હતી. આ બેઠકમાં જીપીસીબી અધ્યક્ષ આર.બી.બારડ, સભ્ય સચિવ ડી.એમ.ઠાકર હાજર રહ્યા હતા.



ગાંધીનગરમાં ઉદ્યોગકારોને કનડતા પ્રશ્નો અંગે બેઠક યોજાઇ
આ બેઠકમાં એસોસિએશનના વિવિધ પ્રશ્નો જેવા કે NCT અપગ્રેડેશન, બુસ્ટર પંપનું ઝડપી અમલીકરણ AWML ગ્રાન્ટ, રૂલ-9માંથી ઘણી બધી વેલ્યુ એડેડ પ્રોડક્ટો જે ડાયરેક્ટ જનરેટ કરી શકાતી હોય, તેમજ Hyderochloric Acid મુદ્દે દરેક ઉદ્યોગને સમાન દરજ્જો અપાય અને HZને બદલે પ્રોડક્ટમાં ગણના થાય, EC NOC ટાઈમ લિમિટ, CEPI-INDEX સ્કોર, અકસ્માતોમાં કંપનીઓને ક્લોઝર-દંડ સહિતના મુદ્દે ઉગ્ર રજૂઆતો મંત્રીઓ સાથે ચર્ચાઓ વિચારણાઓ કરાઈ હતી.

થોડા દિવસ પહેલા મુખ્યમંત્રીને રજૂઆત કરાઈ હતી
બેઠક દરમિયાન ઉપરોક્ત દરેક મહત્વના મુદ્દાઓ ગણી લાંબી ચર્ચા થઈ હતી. ઉદ્યોગકારોએ કરેલી રજૂઆતોને પર્યાવરણ વિભાગે અને ઉદ્યોગ મંત્રીએ વિશેષ ધ્યાન લઇને આવનારા દિવસોમાં તેમની રજૂઆતો હલ કરી જરૂરી કાર્યવાહી કરવાની ખાતરી આપી હતી. અત્રે ઉલ્લેખનીય છે કે, થોડા મહિના અગાઉ ઉદ્યોગકારોએ મુખ્યમંત્રી ભુપેન્દ્ર પટેલને પણ મળીને તેમને ઉદ્યોગોમાં.કનડતા પ્રશ્નોની રજૂઆતો કરી હતી.

આ બેઠકમાં એસોસિએશનના સભ્યો હાજર રહ્યા હતા
આ બેઠકમાં અંકલેશ્વર AIA પ્રમુખ જશુ ચૌધરી, ઉપપ્રમુખ હિંમતભાઇ તેમજ હરેશ પટેલ, માજી પ્રમુખ મહેશભાઈ અને સાથે નટુ પટેલ, પાનોલી PIAના

આ બેઠકમાં એસોસિએશનના વિવિધ પ્રશ્નો જેવા કે nct અપગ્રેડેશન, બુસ્ટર પંપનું ઝડપી અમલીકરણ awml ગ્રાન્ટ, રૂલ-9માંથી ઘણી બધી વેલ્યુ એડેડ પ્રોડક્ટો જે ડાયરેક્ટ જનરેટ કરી શકાતી હોય, તેમજ hyderochloric acid મુદ્દે દરેક ઉદ્યોગને સમાન દરજ્જો અપાય અને hzને બદલે પ્રોડક્ટમાં ગણના થાય, ec noc ટાઈમ લિમિટ, cepi-index સ્કોર, અકસ્માતોમાં કંપનીઓને ક્લોઝર-દંડ સહિતના મુદ્દે ઉગ્ર રજૂઆતો મંત્રીઓ સાથે ચર્ચાઓ વિચારણાઓ કરાઈ હતી.

થોડા દિવસ પહેલા મુખ્યમંત્રીને રજૂઆત કરાઈ હતી
બેઠક દરમિયાન ઉપરોક્ત દરેક મહત્વના મુદ્દાઓ ગણી લાંબી ચર્ચા થઈ હતી. ઉદ્યોગકારોએ કરેલી રજૂઆતોને પર્યાવરણ વિભાગે અને ઉદ્યોગ મંત્રીએ વિશેષ ધ્યાન લઇને આવનારા દિવસોમાં તેમની રજૂઆતો હલ કરી જરૂરી કાર્યવાહી કરવાની ખાતરી આપી હતી. અત્રે ઉલ્લેખનીય છે કે, થોડા મહિના અગાઉ ઉદ્યોગકારોએ મુખ્યમંત્રી ભુપેન્દ્ર પટેલને પણ મળીને તેમને ઉદ્યોગોમાં.કનડતા પ્રશ્નોની રજૂઆતો કરી હતી.

આ બેઠકમાં એસોસિએશનના સભ્યો હાજર રહ્યા હતા
આ બેઠકમાં અંકલેશ્વર aia પ્રમુખ જશુ ચૌધરી, ઉપપ્રમુખ હિંમતભાઇ તેમજ હરેશ પટેલ, માજી પ્રમુખ મહેશભાઈ અને સાથે નટુ પટેલ, પાનોલી piaના

.
*Excellent Quotes by Zaverchand Meghani*😗

*ધીમે ધીમે વૃદ્ધિ પામી* 
*તું વૃધ્ધ થા..,* 
*કાં પછી સર્વસ્વ ત્યાગી* 
*તું બુધ્ધ થા....!!!*

*સ્નાન હો ઘરમાં કે પછી*
*હો ગંગા તટે....,* 
*છે શરત એક જ - કે તું* 
*ભીતરથી શુધ્ધ થા..!!!*

પાણીથી ન્હાય તે
"કપડાં" બદલી શકે છે,
પણ પરસેવે ન્હાય તે
"કિસ્મત" બદલી શકે છે.

પ્રભુ એટલું આપજો,
શોધવું પણ ના પડે,
સંતાડવું પણ ના પડે..!!!

વિચાર ગમે તેટલો
સુંદર હોય તે..,
આચાર વિના નકામો છે..!!

પ્રભુ, હું ક્યાં કહુ છું 
કે તૂ આંગણ સુધી આવ.? 
આંખ મીચું.....ને બસ
પાંપણ સુધી આવ..!
.ઝવેરચંદ  મેઘાણી🙏🙏🙏

notes.text:
Axis Bank
Which is the Most Premium Card of Axis Bank?
CardInsider
23 Feb
Edited
Considering the requirements and needs of every different individual in mind, Axis Bank issues a host of credit cards ranging from entry-level to super-premium offering rewards and benefits across various categories. Super-premium cards target high net-worth individuals and businessmen who live a life of luxury and do not compromise with their elite lifestyle.

Keeping in mind their premium customers, Axis Bank provides super-premium credit cards with a high joining fee that offer top privileges and benefits across travel, dining, shopping, entertainment, lifestyle, etc. Here are the most premium credit cards by Axis Bank –
image

Axis Bank Reserve Credit Card

The most premium offering by Axis Bank is the Reserve credit card which comes with an annual fee of Rs. 50,000 plus taxes. Upon activation of the card, you get a welcome benefit of 50,000 EDGE Reward points, 15 Reward Points on every spend of Rs. 200, and 2x Reward Points on international transactions.

Other than reward points, you get many high-quality privileges like unlimited complimentary lounge access, membership of top hotel loyalty programs, dining and movie benefits, etc. Here is a closer look at what the super-premium Axis Reserve credit card has to offer –

Bonus 50,000 EDGE Reward Points on card activation or renewal
15 Edge Points on every spend of Rs. 200 and 2X Points on all international transactions
Unlimited domestic and international lounge access with Priority Pass membership for primary as well as add-on cardholders. You even get 12 visits every year for guests  no cash withdrawal charges
image

Axis Bank Magnus Credit Card

Another premium credit card offering by Axis Bank is the Magnus credit card which comes at a Rs. 10,000 annual fee. This sought-after card is loved by those who want luxury and offers premium travel, movie, dining. Wellness, entertainment, lifestyle, and insurance benefits. Here is what this premium card offers –

Welcome reward of a complimentary domestic flight ticket or a Rs. 10,000 Tata CLiQ voucher
12 Edge Reward Points on every spend of Rs. 200 on retail and 5X Reward Points on spends on TRAVEL EDGE
Bonus 25,000 Edge Reward points on spending over Rs. 1 Lakh in the previous month
Unlimited domestic and international lounge access with the Priority Pass membership
Airport concierge service including assistance with security checks, check-in, porter services, immigration, etc.
Exclusive discount offers at Oberoi resorts and hotels
BOGO ticket offer on BookMyShow up to a maximum of Rs. 500, 5 times in a month
40% off at over 4000 restaurants in India with the EazyDiner program
Low forex markup fee of 2% and no cash withdrawal charges
Thus, the Axis Bank Reserve credit card is the most premium offering by the bank that comes with a hefty annual fee of Rs. 50,000 plus taxes. If you are on the lookout for premium cards, then the Axis Bank Magnus is another great option. It is fully-loaded with benefits and has a Rs. 10,000 annual fee which is considerably lower than the Reserve credit card.

Open the Registry Editor. Using the combine keys Win+R to open the Run window, enter regedit, and click OK to open the Registry Editor.
Locate IFEO in Registry Editor. On the left side of the Registry Editor, navigate to the following registry key.
Create a new registry key for Notepad. ...
Set the debugger value for Notepad. ...
Check and confirm the test. ...

notes.title: Untitled note
notes.text:
With this routine, which totals 68 seconds a day, you can enhance your mood, become more aware of opportunity, and increase the likelihood of making your vision and dreams happen. Here's how.

Visualize your goals.

Several times a day, stop what you're doing andvisualize, with as much intensity as possible, the strong, positive emotion associated with achieving your ultimate vision. A mere 17 seconds of holding these thoughts and feelings and your mind will begin its transition from a negative state toward an uplifted state. A full 68 seconds increases the power of the thought.

Why does this matter? Because, over time, your brain will form new neural pathways, which strengthen into habits through the repetition and practice of visualizing, feeling, and acting. Therefore, the more frequently you devote 68 seconds to the visualization of your dream come true, the faster you will notice a significant change in your mindset. You'll be more apt to notice, and act upon, opportunities that support your goals and vision. Since like attracts like, positive, successful people will be drawn into your environment. You'll also believe in yourself more and experience more motivation to achieve your goals.

Not visual? No worries. As I walk my clients through this powerful process, some say they aren't good at visualization. If that's true for you, try this alternative approach. This is actually a powerful process that will benefit everyone.

State your goals.

Begin your day by passionately stating your goals for the day aloud, followed by a statement about your vision being your reality now.

Such declarations send the power of your subconscious mind on a mission to find solutions to fulfill your goals. This may have a subtle effect that builds up over time or it may create a powerful change more immediately.

I strongly encourage you to give this a try for about a month, even if you are still a non-believer. See it as an experiment and document your research by keeping a simple journal on your moods and the positive things that occur each day. You can do it; 68 seconds a day will not bankrupt you of time.

notes.text:
*Here is a collection of some superb hard hitting humorous comments on govt, politics, bureaucracy and similar works..*

*"In my many years I have come to a conclusion, ... that one useless man is a shame,   two [useless men] is a law firm and three or more [useless men] is a government."*
*~John Adams*

                               ******
"If you don't read  the newspaper you are  uninformed, if you do
read the newspaper you are  misinformed." 

*~Mark Twain*

                 *******

*"I  contend that for  a nation to try to tax itself into prosperity is like a man standing in a bucket and trying  to lift himself up by the  handle."*
*~Winston Churchill*
                     *********
"A government which  robs Peter to pay Paul can always depend on the support of  Paul."

*~George Bernard Shaw*
                    *******

"Foreign aid might be defined as a transfer of money from poor
people in rich countries to rich people in poor countries."

*~ Douglas Casey, Classmate of Bill Clinton at Georgetown University*

                    ******
"Giving  money and  power to  government is like giving
whiskey and car keys to teenage boys."

*~P.J. O'Rourke,  Civil Libertarian*

                                 ******

*"Just because you do not take an interest in politics doesn't mean politics won't take an interest in you!"*

*~Pericles (430  B.C.)*

                         *******

"No man's life, liberty, or property is safe while the legislature is in session."

~Mark Twain  (1866)
                               

                          ******
*"The government is like a baby's alimentary canal, with a happy appetite at one end and no responsibility at the other."*

*~ Ronald  Reagan*

                        ******
"The  only difference between a tax man and a taxidermist is that the taxidermist leaves the skin."

*~Mark Twain*

                     *******

*"What this country needs are more unemployed politicians."*

*~Edward Langley,  Artist (1928-1995)*
                              ***
"A  government big enough to give you everything you want,is strong enough to take everything you have."

*~Thomas Jefferson*

                          *******
*"We hang the petty thieves and appoint the great ones to public office."*

*~Aesop*
                           ********
"If you think health care is expensive now, wait until you see what it costs when it's free!"

*~P.J.  O'Rourke*

                        ******

notes.title: 7 min work out
notes.text:
The 12 exercises are:

30 sec each with 10 sec break
1) Jumping jacks (full-body warm-up)
2) Wall sit (lower body strength)
3) Push-ups (upper body strength)
4) Abdominal crunches (core strength)
5) Step-ups on a chair (lower body and cardio)
6) Squats (lower body strength)
7) Triceps dips on a chair (upper body strength)
8) Plank (core and stability)
9) High knees running in place (cardio)
10) Lunges (lower body strength)
11) Push-ups with rotation (upper body and core)
12) Side planks (core and stability)

notes.title: Adani
notes.text:
Even the Harvesting of Cauliflower could take anywhere between 75–85 days. But I have heard commercial cultivation takes much lower time through growth-inducing processes which are not very good for health. Consumption of Cauliflower quite often leads to hyperacidity and gas. Adani’s growth or rise is much faster than the commercially cultivated cauliflower. It would definitely cause indigestion. Organically grown cauliflowers are generally not of very big size. The big-size, spotless cauliflowers are quite often products of induced growth. Some of the Adani houses have grown by a few thousand percent over the last five years. So people can understand what kind of growth they could be.

Edit -

I have kept this article very simple. However, someone in the comment section wanted to battle it out. His strength of argument is that foreign banks have invested in the Adani Group. His argument is that international banks can not be oblivious to what an author in Quora would know. Quite a strong argument. However, my response is that the Hindenburg financial report is also from an international organization. Nathan Anderson who founded Hindenburg Research in 2017, would definitely not be oblivious to the fact that international banks also finance the Adani Group enterprises.

Hindenburg Research’s explosive 32,000-word report, which alleges that the Adani Group and its principals have engaged in a years-long scheme of fraud and stock market manipulation. (The Adani Group has denied all wrongdoing and says it is considering legal action against the investment firm.)

In recent years however, U.S and European-based investment banks have stepped up to help the Adani Group raise billions of dollars through equity sales, refinancings and U.S. dollar debt offerings. In addition to the Adani Group’s “relationship” banks, J.P. Morgan, Bank of America Merrill Lynch and Credit Suisse have all brokered deals on behalf of Adani-owned companies.

Between 2015 and 2021, six different Adani Group companies raised about $10 billion through U.S-dollar-denominated bond sales that were underwritten by U.S. and European investment banks, according to financial market data provider Refinitiv. Of these 18 bond offerings, 14 were done between May 2019 and September 2021. One of these companies, Adani Ports & Special Economic Zone - which receives preferential tax treatments - was responsible for half the debt raised.

These figures do not include the Adani Group’s debt issued in Rupees and other currencies. The conglomerate had about $27 billion in outstanding liabilities as of March 2022. The State Bank of India provided funding for about 40% of debt Adani firms issued between 2020 and 2022.

Let’s wait and see whether Adani initiates legal action against the Hindenburg report in the US court. Please remember to fight a legal battle in the US court is not exactly like that in India. In India, you can simply pull it on and on for years. In the US cases are decided much faster and there is no way you can pull it around. Unless of course if Adani somehow manages to put it on the slow paddle pulling some string. If the judgment goes against Adani, it could be disastrous. And I am afraid that Adani could lose the battle.

Meanwhile, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, JP Morgan, and Bank of America all declined to comment on their relationship with the Adani Group. Standard Chartered Bank said it doesn’t comment on client relationships due to confidentiality. And Credit Suisse had not responded at the time of publication. Secondly, we need to wait for more details about these international banks’ dealings to come out. I mean to say who all were guarantors for Adani, was there any counter-guarantee, what nature of the debt was, how much was the bond sale value, what was the margin offered, what was the refinance amount, etc. We don’t need to be jumpy. My only point is that what I said in many of my articles has been endorsed by a report from a US-based international firm. People can refer to my answer given in the reference.

Last but not the least people need to understand that the entire amount Adani Group raised through foreign banks is not in the form of loans. The majority of them are through offshore bond sales. If we get to know who are those buyers the actual story would come out. I am sure there will be some great story to tell if the details are leaked out. Plus these are the way the corporate raiders make hostile takeovers. They will extend you loans, and credits through unsuspecting houses and wait for the right time to make takeover bids. So investment by foreign banks need not be a character certificate for Adani.

notes.title: Belly fat
notes.text:
Eat 50 per cent of your daily calories at lunch as your digestive power is the strongest at that time. Eat the least number of calories at dinner, which should be before 7pm,” he said.

*Reduce consumption of refined carbohydrates; stay away from sweets, sweetened drinks, and foods rich in oil
*Count on these simple tips

*Consume fine powder of roasted fenugreek seeds with water in the morning on an empty stomach or you can soak fenugreek seeds overnight and consume them in the morning on an empty stomach


For many, losing weight may not be the real problem — it is in fact the stubborn belly fat or the fat around the abdomen that they struggle with. While genetic factors may play a role, belly fat can also be controlled with a few lifestyle tweaks, suggested ayurvedic practitioner Dr Shyam VL.

According to him, one should look to ‘melt the belly fat away naturally’ with some effective, easy-to-follow Ayurvedic tips. “Belly fat might look stubborn but with right diet and exercise, you can melt fat easily. Get guidance from an Ayurvedic doctor to help you work with a detailed plan,” he mentioned.


Here’s what you should keep in mind

ADVERTISEMENT
“Eat 50 per cent of your daily calories at lunch as your digestive power is the strongest at that time. Eat the least number of calories at dinner, which should be before 7pm,” he said.

*Reduce consumption of refined carbohydrates; stay away from sweets, sweetened drinks, and foods rich in oil.

ADVERTI

*Consume fine powder of roasted fenugreek seeds with water in the morning on an empty stomach or you can soak fenugreek seeds overnight and consume them in the morning on an empty stomach


*According to Dr Shyam, garcinia cambogia fruit enhances taste, improves digestion and boosts metabolism which helps lose weight.

*Triphala helps eliminate toxins from the body and rejuvenates the digestive system.

How to use? Take a teaspoon of triphala powder with warm water two hours post-dinner.

*Dry ginger powder contains thermogenic agents that are useful to burn fat. Regular consumption of water boiled with dried ginger powder may actually help in boosting your metabolism and burning off the excess fat. You can also add ginger to your daily diet to aid weight loss.

*Brisk walking by holding the core of your tummy for 30 minutes is an effective method for burning belly fat, said Dr Shyam. Yoga and Pilates also help burn belly weight.

*Whenever you are thirsty, drink warm water. Warm water activates your metabolism and aids in weight loss.

*Take time to chew food. “Carbohydrate digestion starts in the mouth whilst mixing with saliva. Chewing helps break down food in the mouth before it travels to the rest of the digestive tract. It also activates the satiety hormone alerting when the stomach is full,” he said.

notes.title: Cardiac
notes.text:
Jaslok hospital 17 floor cath lab 


People also ask
What are the indications for Bentall?
Current indications for an elective Bentall procedure include an ascending aortic diameter of 6 cm or greater, with significant aortic valvular dysfunction, and dilatation of the ascending aorta greater than 5 cm in patients with Marfan syndrome or a bicuspid aortic valve.

Repair of the ascending aorta/root is recommended when the aortic diameter is ≥55 mm in patients without risk factors

Best thali in Chennai, as picked by the city’s top foodies
THE TOP PLACES TO INDULGE IN VEGETARIAN AND NON-VEGETARIAN MEALS IN CHENNAI
TOSHITA SAHNI
28 December 2022
Travelling to Chennai is incomplete without trying the delicious meals the city has to offer. For the uninitiated, what we call thalis are more commonly known as “meals” in Chennai. Just like a thali, a meal is an assortment of mouth-watering delicacies, including rice and side dishes. While traditional restaurants serve them on a banana leaf, others have switched to using plates. Most outlets have a strict no-sharing policy, so come prepared to finish the entire feast by yourself! 

Picking one place on your visit can be a challenge, so we asked food experts and local foodies to pick their favourites in the city. Here are their top picks for vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals:


Best thalis/meals in Chennai
Amravathi, Cathedral Road and Nair’s Mess, Triplicane
“For non-vegetarian meals, my favourite spots are Amravathi and Nair’s Mess. Amravathi (1, Cathedral Rd, opp. Music Academy, Parthasarathypuram, Mylapore) serves Andhra-style meals and is unparalleled for how well they do their non-vegetarian meals. The meat is incredibly well done and cooked in really flavourful masalas. It’s spicy and super hearty. They have vegetarian options too. I love Nair’s Mess (Plot No. 22, Mohammed Abdullah 2nd Street, Chepauk) for its seafood. The prawn masala at Nair’s will always have my heart as it’s so well-balanced. I also enjoy their fish fry. Their flavours are gentle but very well rounded.” Adhira Swami,  chef and culinary consultant

Manjal, Teynampet
“Manjal (6/9, Kasthuri Estate, 3rd St, Poes Garden) has amazing food and a great setup. It has everything you want in a good South Indian non-vegetarian thali: different meats cooked with local flavours. What else could a meat lover ask for?” Abbas Shahzad, founder, Urban Gluttony Co.

“Manjal’s USP is that they bring you a massive tray of individual portions of about 15 appetisers that you can choose from. You can follow this up with the thali that perfectly complements the appetisers. But beware, overeating is almost inevitable.” Payal Rajankar, chef and founder, The Gourmet Gig


Dakshin, Crowne Plaza, Alwarpet
“For the higher-end thalis, I would say Dakshin (Crowne Plaza Chennai Adyar Park, 132, TT Krishnamachari Rd, Austin Nagar, Alwarpet) is my go-to; it’s an institution. The number of sides they serve is amazing as you get to taste so many different South Indian preparations from different regions; their banana dosa is most definitely an all-time favourite.” Adhira Swami, chef and culinary consultant

The thali at Dakshin
The thali at Dakshin
“The South Indian thalis at Dakshin are a great way to explore the culinary repertoire of this restaurant with a three-decade legacy. For me, it’s also probably nostalgia, as this was a preferred venue for family celebrations.” Ashwin Rajagopalan, consultant and lifestyle writer

Ponnusamy, Nungambakkam
“You will get one of the best meat thalis in Chennai at Ponnusamy (7, Jaganathan Road, Ponnangipuram, Tirumurthy Nagar, Nungambakkam). This is a traditional Chettinad restaurant, with a great vegetarian and non-vegetarian thali. Their Bahubali thali is an extravagant feature that you cannot ignore. If you walk into their Nungambakkam branch (the only one that serves this) at any given time, you're sure to find a couple of tables that have ordered this. Consisting of over 50 dishes that include shark, fish, chicken, mutton, prawn, biryani, vegetarian food and dessert. It's great to experience

Erode Amman Mess
“For delicious Tamil food, go to Erode Amman Mess (Multiple branches; Instagram). They offer limited meals with fish curry, fish fry and chicken chukka. They also have one of the best kalakkis in town.” Mathangi Kumar, chef and culinary consultant

Royal Vega, ITC Grand Chola, Guindy
“I would recommend the thali at Royal Vega (63, Anna Salai, Little Mount, Guindy), the restaurant inside ITC Grand Chola, purely for the experience. It’s a proper luxury meal, with great flavours and textures. I especially loved the smoked beetroot patties.” Abbas Shahzad,  founder, Urban Gluttony Co.

Pattukottai Kamatchi Mess, T. Nagar
“Pattukottai Kamatchi Mess (4, Singaravelu St, Opposite EB, Pondy Bazaar, Parthasarathi Puram, T. Nagar) is a pure meat lover's delight. They serve meals with seven non-vegetarian gravies, rice and South Indian

notes.id: 419-coagulant is added to remove coagulatable heavy m
notes.sourceFile: coagulant is added to remove coagulatable heavy m.json
notes.title:
coagulant is added to remove coagulatable heavy metal ion from the system. It is preferred, that between 10 to 200 ppm be added after the pH of the system has been adjust to within the range of 8 and 10 and preferrably within the range of 81/2 and 91/2. It is most preferred that between 50 to 115 ppm be added. Generally any coagulant utilized in water treatment is usable, included among these are diallyldimethyl ammonium chloride polymer, epichlorohydrin-dimethylamine polymers aka Epi-DMA polymers, ethylene amine homopolymer, polyaluminum chloride and alum.

The coagulant used in the Examples is Coagulant A which is a mixture of polyaluminum chloride (based on 7.4% Al2 O3) and 1.5% Epi-DMA with the remainder being water and approximately 1 to 2 weight percent stabilizer for the coagulant composition.

In practice, the surfactant should preferrably be added to the system then the system is adjusted to a pH within the range of 8-10. Preferably between 37 to 148 ppm of surfactant ut

notes.title: computer commands  IT
notes.text:
CHKDSK: A command to help with disk issues 
 
SFC /SCANNOW: A command to repair system files 
 
DISM: A command for the Deployment Image Service and Management Tool 
 
NETSH INT IP RESET: A command to help with internet connection issues 
 
POWERCFG: A command to manage power settings 
 
TASKLIST and TASKKILL: Commands to help tame resource-hogging processes 
 
MSCONFIG: A command to uncheck unnecessary programs that automatically launch when you start your computer 
 
Disk Cleanup: A software tool that removes unnecessary clutter from hard drives, such as temporary files and cached web pages 
 
Restart your computer: Restarting your computer can refresh memory and allow for proper installations to update 
 
Defragment your hard drive: A simple way to improve your computer's speed by bringing together the different sections of your files into the smallest possible sequential area on your disk 
 
Disable startup programs: Disable unused programs that run at startup 
 
Disable special effects: Disable special effects, such as translucency and animations 
 
Turn off search indexing: Turn off search indexing if your PC is slower

notes.title: Current cardiac issues
notes.text:
1) Sound of the valve wood knocking as against click clack or clock ticking sound of other patients, 
2) Heart appears to be beating harder (not faster)
3) transient Pain in a straight line from heart to stomach left side
4) Mild swelling in the legs , ( socks leave marks ), but no major  pitting edema,  or dent  on pressing with a finger  
5) low grade consistent Pain in the back between the shoulder blades.
6) Pain below right collar bone ( transient ) 
7) I am regularly walk up to 4 km continuously without any breathlessness,  3-4 times a week , but sometimes feel breathless especially while talking 
8) my heartbeat becomes louder when I breathe in deeply. 
9) I have started swimming , 



These criteria classify the LV size as
 normal - 42 to 59 mm 
mildly dilated  60 to 63
 moderately dilated 64 to 68 mm; 
 severely dilated (men: ≥69 mm)

LVEF

notes.title: Dahej Yashvi
notes.text:
Dear Sir, 
  
Our manufacturing plant Yashshavi Rasayan is located at SEZ 2 Dahej. 
  
We are looking for a solution for MEE condensate........ 
  
Please find below the parameters for our MEE condensate 
  
1. COD is 8000-10000 ppm 
2. TDS  is 1000-3000   ppm 
3. Methanol is 6000-10000 ppm 
4. 2,5 Di Chloro Phenol is 0-150 ppm 
5.orthoxylene/methyl ester/anisole are having solubility of 1000 ppm as combined 
6.PH is 5.0 to 8.0 
7.Condensate generation is 200-250 KL/Day 
  
We are currently having 02 ozonators of 600gm/Hr ozone capacity 
  
We want to have COD less than 250 ppm,TDS less than 200 ppm,other organic zero,ph 7 to 8 
  
Please let us know if you have suitable membranes for this application and can you do pilot plant trials. 
  
Regards 
Nilesh Merai 
9099790493 / 6353678389

notes.title: Diet for good cholesterol HDL
notes.text:
Sample 3-Day Indian Diet Plan for Cholesterol
Day 1
Early morning: Soaked methi seeds (1 tbsp) in warm water
Breakfast: Oats poha with vegetables and a bowl of curd
Mid-morning: Fruit chaat ( grapes, guava, apple)
Lunch: Whole wheat roti, vegetable salad, chicken curry, and cucumber raita
Mid-afternoon: A handful of nuts
Evening snacks: Steamed sprouts
Dinner: Moong dal khichdi,palak sabzi, and curd
Day 2
Early morning: A glass of lemon-infused water
Breakfast: Oats porridge and a bowl of pomegranate with seeds
Mid-morning: An apple and a cup of green tea
Lunch: Brown rice with lauki dal, salads, and buttermilk
Mid-afternoon: Low-fat yoghurt
Evening snacks: Roasted chana
Dinner: Whole wheat roti, gobi matar curry and cucumber raita
Day 3
Early morning: Almonds
Breakfast: Methi roti/thepla, bowl of curd, egg scramble
Mid-morning: 1 Apple or guava
Lunch: Whole wheat roti, palak paneer, vegetable salad
Mid-afternoon: Handful of nuts
Evening snacks: Boiled kala chana dal chat
Dinner: Jowar roti, Methi dal, beans and carrot sabzi

notes.title: Dietician recommendation
notes.text:
1) avoid eating refined flour and bakery products 
2) atta : add bajra and jovar and ragi in wheat 
3) fruits to avoid : grapes , banana, mango, sitaphal, watermelon, muskmelon 
4) fruits to eat: apple, papaya, guava, jamun, pineapple, orange sweet lime, pomegranate. 
5) pre-soaked methi dana empty stomach 
6) cabbage, cauliflower broccoli to limit 
7) water intake 10-12 glasses per day

notes.title: Excel formula for calculating percentage
notes.text:
o calculate the percentage increase or decrease between two columns in Excel, you can use the following formula:

 = ((New Value - Old Value) / ABS(Old Value)) * 100 

For example, if your old value is in cell A1 and the new value is in cell B1, the formula would be:

 = ((B1 - A1) / ABS(A1)) * 100 

This formula will calculate the percentage increase or decrease between the old and new values. If the result is positive, it signifies an increase percentage, and if the result is negative, it represents a

notes.title: Exercise walking
notes.text:
Here's a quick breakdown of step counts to help you measure your walking prowess:
Beginner: If you're hitting 90–110 steps in a minute, you're starting to build a solid walking pace. This range suggests decent cardiovascular health but room for improvement.
Intermediate: Clocking 120–130 steps per minute indicates a brisk walking speed, showcasing muscular endurance and an efficient stride.
Advanced: If you can hit 140+ steps in 60 seconds, you're walking at an elite pace. This reflects excellent cardiovascular health, strong legs, and impressive coordination.

notes.title: Financial Rules
notes.text:
*NINE PERSONAL FINANCE RULES WE ALL MUST KNOW*

1) Rule of 72 (Double Your Money)
2) Rule of 70 (Inflation)
3) 4% Withdrawal Rule
4) 100 Minus Age Rule
5) 10, 5, 3 Rule
6) 50-30-20 Rule
7) 3X Emergency Rule
8) 40℅ EMI Rule
9) Life Insurance Rule

1) *Rule of 72*

No. of yrs required to double your money at a given rate, U just divide 72 by interest rate
Eg, if you want to know how long it will take to double your money at 8% interest, divide 72 by 8 and get 9 years.

At 6% rate, it will take 12 years
At 9% rate, it will take 8 years

2) *Rule of 70*

Divide 70 by current inflation rate to know how fast the value of your investment will get reduced to half its present value. 

Inflation rate of 7% will reduce the value of your money to half in 10 years.

3) *4% Rule for Financial Freedom*

Corpus Required = 25 times of your estimated Annual Expenses.

Eg- if your annual expense after 50 years of age is 500,000 and you wish to take VRS then corpus with you required is 1.25 crore.

Put 50% of this into fixed income & 50% into equity.

Withdraw 4% every year, i.e.5 lacs.

This rule works for 96% of time in 30 years period

4) *100 minus your age rule*

This rule is used for asset allocation. Subtract your age from 100 to find out, how much of your portfolio should be allocated to equities

Suppose your Age is 30 so (100 - 30 = 70)

Equity : 70%
Debt : 30%

But if your Age is 60 so (100 - 60 = 40)

Equity : 40%
Debt : 60%

5) *10-5-3 Rule*

One should have reasonable returns expectations

10℅ Rate of return - Equity / Mutual Funds
5℅ - Debts ( Fixed Deposits or Other Debt instruments) 
3℅ - Savings Account

6) *50-30-20 Rule - about allocation of income to expense*

Divide your income into
50℅ - Needs  (Groceries, rent, emi, etc)
30℅ - Wants / Desires (Entertainment, vacations, etc)
20℅ - Savings  (Equity, MFs, Debt, FD, etc)

At least try to save 20℅ of your income. You can definitely save more...

7) *3X Emergency Rule*

Always put atleast 3 times your monthly income in Emergency funds for emergencies such as Loss of employment, medical emergency, etc. 

3 X Monthly Income

In fact, one can have around 6 X Monthly Income in liquid or near liquid assets to be on a safer side.

8). *40℅ EMI Rule*

Never go beyond 40℅ of your income into EMIs. 

Say if you earn, ₹ 50,000 per month. Then you should not have EMIs more than ₹ 20,000 .

This Rule is generally used by Finance companies to provide loans. You can use it to manage your finances. 

9) *Life Insurance Rule*

Always have Sum Assured as 20 times of your Annual Income. 

20 X Annual Income

Say you earn ₹ 5 Lacs annually, yoy should atleast have 1 crore insurance by following this Rule.

*These rules are equally useful for young, youth and old. Hope you will find them simple, useful and handy.*

notes.title: Garbage orchid
notes.text: Dear All there is too much garbage dumping taking place outside our society we are now practically living inside a garbage dump there is a lot of non-vegetarian meat waste being dumped along the side wall of Orchid Bungalows, the smell is nauseating and all-pervasive, for all the houses alongside the boundary wall towards the side of north of orchid bungalows,  the meat waste that is being dumped regularly is carried by birds and dropped of in the nearby houses alongside orchid bungalows,  resulting in a shower of chicken and meat waste on all these houses ... these has been happening for years now,  and I am afraid that now all the residents of orchid  appear to have become used to living like this.. ..,

notes.title: Health cholesterol
notes.text:
​Celery, cucumber, ginger& mint juice

HS CRP test good for preventing heart attacks..

In general: A total cholesterol level of less than 200 mg/dL (5.17 mmol/L) is normal. A total cholesterol level of 200 to 239 mg/dL (5.17 to 6.18 mmol/L) is borderline high. A total cholesterol level of 240 mg/dL (6.21 mmol/L) or greater is high.

Your HDL (“good” cholesterol) is the one number you want to be high (ideally above 60). Your LDL (“bad” cholesterol) should be below 100. Your total should be below 200.

Jawar, bajra , gehoon best for uric acid....

notes.title: Health
notes.text:
Fret not, grab a bowl of fruits and pick out the cholesterol-friendly ones. We list the top five best fruits that can help manage high cholesterol well.

1)Bananas: The seedless and versatile fruit works well in reducing and regulating high cholesterol levels. A rich source of potassium and soluble fibre, bananas are key to healthy body weight and a strong immune system.

2) Grapes: The bite-sized treats are rich in vitamin C which not only supports the immune system but also helps carry bad cholesterol levels to the liver where it is processed and eventually removed.

3) Apples: Often considered one of the healthiest fruits for high cholesterol woes, apples are a rich source of soluble fibres which make them a heart-friendly choice for all. Apples also contain polyphenols that help manage cholesterol well.

4) Avocados: Despite being extremely costly in India, one cannot deny that avocados are a great way to manage cholesterol levels in the blood. Try adding it to morning toast or smoothies or sandwiches or salads.

5) Pineapple: Pineapple contains bromelain, a compound that breaks down cholesterol deposits in the arteries to support healthy blood flow and beat heart disease risk eventually.

notes.title: Home loan
notes.text:
4-20-30-20

estimations 

 Rule no 1 :  the rule of 4=value of the house should be <4 times anual salary

annual salary is 1 Cr value of the house is  land = 3000 X 7000= 2,10,00,000/- 
cost of making the house another 2-3 Cr , cut to.cut matching 
 
20= down payment

assuming 4 Cr as the net cost of acquisition then  20% of 4 Cr = 80akhs down payment, 

or for land acquisition:   the down payment should be 40 laks, 

30=monthly emi should be less tha 30 %of salary 


20= tenure of home loan should be <20 years...

tenure has to be less than 10 years , 
if emi= 1 k per lakhs then for a loan of 3.20 Cr emi will be 3,20,000 which is not affordable: X2 probably 

verdict as on August 2024 this is not feasible...

notes.title: India venture capital
notes.text:
chapter of non-profit The Indus Entrepreneurs (TiE), which helps start-ups raise funds and network. “Knowing Anandan’s deep commitment to supporting young tech start-ups, this seems like a perfect union. Sequoia and entrepreneurs in their network will find immense value just as we do with his leadership at TiE Delhi-National Capital Region (NCR). A great team leader, he leaves a rich legacy at Google,” said Geetika Dayal, executive director, TiE Delhi-NCR.

He has invested in Popxo, Capillary Technologies, Goonj, and Instamojo, among others. He is also the co-founder of Blue Ocean Ventures, the first seed fund in Sri Lanka.

notes.title: Intro international
notes.text:
I have read with interest the technological innovations of your UF system mentioned in your website 

        We are an Indian company,  dealing in wastewater treatment chemicals and equipment,  we specialize in wastewater recycling with RO,  we have our offices in Baroda and Ankleshwar  in western India, in the middle of what is known as the “ Golden Industrial Corridor”  which runs from Ahmadabad to Bombay ( Mumbai), 

     We have our own office , go-down and laboratory for testing of wastewater,  we also operate and maintain industrial wastewater treatment plants,  we have been in this business for more than 20 years,  and have a deep insights into these markets. 
Our staff is well experienced in pilot plant trials, erection and commissioning of equipment,  operation and maintenance ( on 24 X 7 basis ) , laboratory and plant scale trials,  apart from sales and marketing of chemicals and equipments.
We are operating and maintaining industrial utilities and currently, we have more than 200 RO plants running on treated industrial effluents, 
I have gone through your website and the technologies mentioned are of great interest to us . 
We would like to represent your firm in India. 
     I request you to kindly send us more details and your commercial terms to further our business relationship. 

With regards

Chandan Sarkar

notes.title: Lithium battery fires
notes.text: Small lithium-ion batteries can be doused with water because they contain little lithium metal. Lithium-metal battery fires can be put out with a Class D fire extinguisher. Larger battery fires are best handled with a foam extinguisher, CO2, ABC dry chemical, powder graphite, copper powder or sodium carbonate.

notes.title: Lung boosting tea
notes.text:
An easy lung health boosting tea:

● 1 tbsp methi seeds
● 10g fresh tulsi leaves or 1 tbsp dried tulsi.
● 2 pods cardamom/elaichi (green)
● 1 to 2 tsp saunf/fennel seeds
● Boil all of the above in 1 litre of water
● Reduce it to 500 ml of water
● Strain and sip warm

notes.title: MKS
notes.text:
Design Drg should drawn as per certain IS or ASME code.
- Fabrication / Construction guidelines/ directions should be given in the Drg.
- DP check of all Weld bevel groves, T joint RT / C Ray 
- Wles joint design as per IS / ASME codes 
- No Pad plates given on the Structural Stand/ directly welded to Dished End - Gross violation of any Storage tank or Pressure Vessel
- Pad plates need to have a 1/8 “ vent hole 
- Although it is water reset we tank ,Anti-corrosive paint needs to be given after sandblasting 
-
If the water is corrosive ( Ankleshwar or GIFC water - Inside Epoxy coating required before the start of Fabrication since this is a CS Vessel/ tank) 
- Dor Water tank also Pressure needs to be done either at 7/8 Bar or Vaccum test to be done - to be mentioned in the design drawing. 
- T joint should be FPW with Radiography test post welding.
- weight of equipment ( empty tank not given ), 
Material IS 2062 GrB min structural steel to be taken up, material TC also needs to be collected from the supplier.

notes.title: Mutual funds for 2023
notes.text:
Top-3 mid-cap funds recommendations by Anil Singhvi
1. HDFC Mid Cap Opportunities Fund 
2. Nippon India Growth Fund 
3. Tata Mid Cap Growth Fund

HDFC Mid Cap Opportunities Fund 


The NAV of HDFC Midcap Opportunity Fund as on January 5 is Rs 150.

The Asset Under Management (AUM) is Rs 52,138 crore.

It has given returns of about 60 per cent on net basis to SIP investors in three years and 112 per cent in five years.

If an investor had started a SIP of Rs 10,000 three years ago, the value of that fund today would have been Rs 5.72 lakh.

The total amount of investment would have been Rs 3.60 lakh.

Nippon India Growth Fund 
As on January 5, the NAV of Nippon India Growth Fund is Rs 3278.

The fund size (AUM) is Rs 21380 crore.

It has given returns of about 61 per cent on net basis to SIP investors in three years and 117 per cent in five years.

If an investor had started a SIP of Rs 10,000 three years ago, the value of that fund today would have been Rs 5.78 lakh. 

Tata Midcap Growth Fund
As on January 5, the NAV of Tata Midcap Growth Fund is Rs 357.

The fund size is Rs 2852 crore.

It has given returns of about 49 per cent on net basis to SIP investors in three years and 95 per cent in five years.

If an investor had started a SIP of Rs 10,000 three years ago, the value of that fund today would have been Rs 5.35 lakh.

The total amount of investment would have been Rs 3.6 lakh.

notes.title: Nicholsan Macbride online test psychology grit  test for resilience
notes.text:
test to see how mentally strong you are : 
Nicholson Macbride resilience 
What the Test Reveals:

🔹 Resilience: Learn how well you cope and bounce back from difficult circumstances.

🔹 Stress Management: Discover how skilled you are in coping with stress and regulating your emotions.

Where to Take the Test:

- **MindTools.com**: Take the resilience test online at MindTools.com.

- **Nicholson McBride Resilience Questionnaire**: Find and take the Nicholson McBride Resilience Questionnaire.

Why It’s Important:

Understanding your mental strength helps you identify areas for growth and improvement. It can guide you in developing better coping mechanisms and enhancing your resilience.

notes.title: PE  investments in our customers
notes.text:
1) BlackStone : aquired Piramal Glass for 1 billion 

2) CVC Capital :   aquired Sajjan India  for 7000 plus Cr 

3) KKR : JB Chemicals 

JB Chemicals :  54% stake in JB Chemicals for Rs 3,100 crore

4) Advent International : 

•	ZCL : ZCL was advised by Jefferies (Sole Financial Advisor), Bombay Law Chambers (Legal Counsel), and Deloitte (Finance and Tax). Advent picked up a 51 per cent stake in the entity, which has an enterprise value of Rs 1,700 crore. 

In addition to ZCL, investment in the past 12 months includes RA Chem, a vertically integrated pharmaceutical company; Bharat Serums and Vaccines (BSV), a biopharmaceutical leader in women’s health and critical care; and Aditya Birla Capital, the holding company of the financial services businesses of Aditya Birla Group (ABG), an Indian multinational conglomerate.

notes.title: PT values when or warfin 2-3
notes.text: The aim of warfarin therapy is often to achieve an INR between 2.0 and 3.0 for basic blood-thinning needs or between 2.5 and 3.5 for those with a high risk of clot formation. An INR of 1.193 is in the normal range for someone not on anticoagulation medication; however, it may be considered low if the person is on warfarin.

notes.title: Rich vrs poor
notes.text:
First of all, understand that :

Wealth does not mean having a fat bank account. Wealth is primarily the ability to create wealth.

Example: Someone who wins the lottery or gambling. Even if he wins 100 millions is not a rich man: He is a poor man with a lot of money.  That's the reason why 90% of the lottery millionaires become poor again after 5 years.

You also have rich people who have no money.
Example: Most entrepreneurs.
THEY are already on the road to wealth even though they have no money, because they are developing their financial intelligence and that is wealth. 

How are the rich and the poor different?

To put it simply: The rich may die to become rich, while the poor may kill to become rich.

If you see a young person who decides to train, to learn new things, who tries to improve himself constantly, know that he is a rich man.

If you see a young person who thinks that the problem is the state, and who thinks that the rich are all thieves and who criticizes constantly, know that he is a poor man.

The rich are convinced that they just need information and training to take off, the poor think that others must give them money to take off.

 I am not talking about money. I'm talking about the ability to create wealth in that man.

most criminals are poor people. When they are in front of money, they lose their mind, that's why they rob, steal etc... For them it is a grace because they don't know how they could earn money by themselves.

One day, the guard of a bank found a bag full of money, he took the bag and went to give it to the bank manager.

People called this man an idiot, but in reality this man was just a rich man who had no money.


Wealth is first of all a state of mind.

*So … are u rich or poor?*

notes.title: RUBIK'S CUBE -Noah Richadson
notes.text:
RUBIK'S CUBE 

1) White is up - RUR' U'  RUR'U'  RUR 

2) White is facing you  -  U'R U'R' U2 RUR 

3) White is to the right - DW R'UR U2 R'UR 

solving the yellow side
1) Yellow bar :  FRU R'U'F' 
2) yellow hook : FUR U'R'F'
3) Yellow Cross : The ZUNE move:  RUR' UR U2R 

R'FR' B2 RF'R' B2R2 - matching corners go to the back side
M2U M2U2 M2UM2
M2UM2 UM' U2M2U2 M'U2 

R2-URU R'U'R' U'R'UR' 
RU'- RUR UR U'R'U' R2 - CUBE SOLVED

By implementing SCALE-GUARD™ technology, Nalco Water scale control can help to maximize productivity and water availability while protecting processing equipment and minimizing wear and tear. Nalco Water's scale control portfolio can help reduce the total cost of your operations and increase your overall profitability.

notes.title: sechengen visa

listItems.text: Must have Documents for Netherlands Visaexpand_more
Original Passport with at least 6 months validity and minimum 3 blank pages + all old passports if any;
Personal Covering letter (For Employed - Plain paper/ For Self-Employed – Company Letterhead);
Income Tax Returns / Form 16 for last 3 years;
Kindly note: The Govt is very strict on the photograph requirement; please ensure that your photos are as per the specifications.

notes.preview: Must have Documents for Netherlands Visaexpand_more • Original Passport with at least 6 months validity and minimum 3 blank pages + all old passports if any; • Visa Application for
notes.searchText: sechengen visa must have documents for netherlands visaexpand_more original passport with at least 6 months validity and minimum 3 blank pages + all old passports if any; visa application form; 2 recent colour photographs (photo specification); personal covering letter (for employed - plain paper/ for self-employed – company letterhead); original updated bank statement (last 6 months); income tax returns / form 16 for last 3 years; flight reservation; hotel reservation; day wise itinerary; travel insurance (minimum coverage of eur 30000). kindly note: the govt is very strict on the photograph requirement; please ensure that your photos are as per the specifications.

notes.title: Selenium thyroid foods
notes.text:
Source:

1. Brazil nuts
Brazil nuts are very high in selenium, with 1 ounce (oz), or 6–8 nuts, containing 544 mcg. That’s 989% of an adult’s recommended daily value (DV).

2. Tuna
Around 3 oz of cooked, yellowfin tuna contains 92 mcg of selenium, or 167% of the adult DV.

3. Halibut
The same size portion of halibut has 47 mcg, or 85% of the adult DV.

4. Sardines
Once drained, a 3 oz can of sardines in oil, with bones, will account for 82% of the adult DV. That’s because it contains 45 mcg of selenium.

5. Roasted ham
A 3 oz portion of roasted ham contains 42 mcg of selenium. That equates to 76% of the adult DV.

6. Shrimp
Around 3 oz of canned shrimp has 40 mcg of selenium, or 73% of the adult DV.

7. Enriched macaroni
Some brands of macaroni are enriched with selenium. Once cooked, one cup of this type of pasta will contain 37 mcg, or 67% of the adult DV.

8. Turkey
A 3 oz portion of boneless, roasted turkey contains 56% of the adult DV, which is 31 mcg.

9. Beef liver
Pan fried, 3 oz of beef liver can provide 28 mcg, or 51% of an adult’s DV.

10. Chicken
The light meat in chicken contains the selenium. People should aim for around 3 oz of roasted chicken to consume 22 mcg, or 40% of the adult DV.

11. Cottage cheese
One cup of 1% milk fat cottage cheese contains 20 mcg, or 36% of the adult DV.

12. Brown rice
Around one cup of cooked, brown, long-grain rice contains 35% of the adult DV, or 19 mcg.

13. Eggs
Eggs are also a good source of selenium. One large, hard-boiled egg will provide 15 mcg of the nutrient. That equates to 27% of the adult DV.

14. Bread
Bread can boost selenium levels, particularly if a person opts for brown varieties. On average, a slice of whole-wheat bread contains 24% of the adult DV, or 13 mcg.

15. Baked beans
Baked beans are a good

notes.title: Small cup mutual funds
notes.text:
Three small cap funds have offered more than 40% in three years, an analysis of performance of mutual funds showed. Quant Small Cap Fund, the topper in the category, offered 44.01% in the three year horizon. Nippon India Small Cap Fund gave 42.25%. HSBC Small Cap Fund offered 40.38% in a three-year horizon.

The small cap category has offered an average return of 34.91% in the three-year period. Around 21 small cap funds have completed three years of existence in the market. Around 15 small cap schemes gave returns ranging 31.04-38.42% in the same time period. ITI Small Cap Fund offered the lowest return of around 26.07%.

notes.title: Smoothie
notes.text:
1 cup spinach (palak) 
¼ cup mint leaves (pudina) 
¼ cup coriander leaves (dhania) 
1 green apple  
½ tsp lemon juice 
¼ inch ginger, optional 
Salt to taste 
Ice cubes 


2)  Recipe: Basic green smoothie
Ingredients: 
2 bananas, frozen

1 cup spinach

1 cup coconut water

2-3 dates, optional, as per sweetness required

Method: 
Freeze the bananas overnight or for at least 6 hours. This will give your smoothie a rich texture.

Blend the frozen bananas, dates, spinach and coconut water into a smooth consistency and add ice. Serve immediately.

2. Recipe: Vegetable green smoothie
Ingredients: 
1 cup spinach

½ cup mint leaves

A handful coriander

A few curry leaves

2-3 stalks celery

A few basil leaves

1 green cucumber

2 tbsp ash gourd/bottle gourd

½ cup pineapple, optional

1 lemon for lemon juice

2-3 dates

notes.title: tech gifts
notes.text:
Eero
This clever take on the router knows that in this era, the best gift possible is speedy, reliable WiFi. Eero’s advanced system covers every spot of your home (backyard included) in a Wi-Fi “mesh,” with a simple plug-and-play installation. Instead of calling their Internet provider if something goes wrong, your friends can troubleshoot this tech gift via a user-friendly app—or get support from Eero’s team in an average of 60 seconds.
 Zoom Cellphone Camera Lens
This optical zoom lens can transform your phone into a widescreen version of binoculars, finally justifying its rightful place on, say, a safari vacation (when not attached to the phone, it can be used as a monocle, too). A manual focusing ring lets the tech lover you're shopping for adjust to get the sharpest image possible.

Fitbit Flyer Wireless Headphones
Now that Apple has eliminated the headphone jack, the search is on for the perfect pair of Bluetooth headphones that are stylish and comfortable for listening to your favorite music on the treadmill to your desk. Enter the Fitbit Flyer headphones: a sleek, sweat-proof set that comes in two colors with an adjustable cable, a built-in microphone, and more high-tech fe

notes.title: The 12 exercises in the 7-minute workout
notes.text:
The 12 exercises in the 7-minute workout target all the body's major muscle groups:

Jumping jacks (total body)
Wall sit (lower body)
Push-up (upper body)
Abdominal crunch (core)
Step-up onto chair (total body)
Squat (lower body)
Triceps dip on chair (upper body)
Plank (core)
High knees /running in place (total body)
Lunge (lower body)

notes.title: The Mumbai Canteen
notes.text:
Food join in mumbai speciality : 

Some of the cocktails that you must try at The Bombay Canteen include the Tar Booze (watermelon juice, vodka, and kala namak), the Jhoom Jhaam (a concoction of pandan-infused vodka, apricot jam, white chocolate syrup, and orange bitters), Liberty Cinema (Raspberry –infused Gordon’s gin, lime, Chandon Sparkling Wine), and Incredible India (Smirnoff vodka, fresh ginger, pineapple juice, basil leaves, orange juice).

The tantalizing dishes to be tried include the Desi Taco - Goan Pulled Pork Vindaloo, My Uncle's Mutton Curry, Kejriwal Toasts, Gulab Nut, Red Snapper Ceviche, Guava Tan Ta Tan, Fresh Toor Samosa Chaat, Veg Mutton Kofta and Canteen Haleem.

some of the cocktails that you must try at the bombay canteen include the tar booze (watermelon juice, vodka, and kala namak), the jhoom jhaam (a concoction of pandan-infused vodka, apricot jam, white chocolate syrup, and orange bitters), liberty cinema (raspberry –infused gordon’s gin, lime, chandon sparkling wine), and incredible india (smirnoff vodka, fresh ginger, pineapple juice, basil leaves, orange juice).

the tantalizing dishes to be tried include the desi taco - goan pulled pork vindaloo, my uncle's mutton curry, kejriwal toasts, gulab nut, red snapper ceviche, guava tan ta tan, fresh toor samosa chaat, veg mutton kofta and canteen haleem.

notes.title: throat pain
notes.text:
Pain relievers, such as Tylenol (acetaminophen), can help decrease pain from a sore throat and possible associated fever.
Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, such as Advil/Motrin (ibuprofen), Aleve (naproxen) or Bayer (aspirin), can also help alleviate pain from a sore throat. However, aspirin is not a safe option for children or teenagers due to the risk of Reye’s syndrome. This rare condition has been linked to aspirin use in children who have been sick with a viral illness. Without prompt treatment, it can lead to fatal swelling in the liver and brain.
Decongestants, can help break up mucus which may contribute to a sore throat. Decongestants come in oral forms, such as Sudafed (pseudoephedrine), or nasal sprays, such as Afrin (oxymetazoline). Keep in mind, Afrin use should be limited to no more than 3 days to prevent rebound congestion.
Throat sprays, such as Cepacol (dyclonine) or Chloraseptic (phenol), help numb the throat and decrease pain from a sore throat.
Cough suppressants, such as Delsym (dextromethorphan), helps control coughing. Medicines like Mucinex (guaifenesin) can thin out phlegm, making it easier to cough up.
Prescription medication

notes.title: Top 5 mutual funds 2025
notes.text:
#1 – Quant Small Cap Fund – 3-Year Return: 215%

#2 – Nippon India Small Cap Fund – 3-Year Return: 180%

#3 – ICICI Prudential BHARAT 22 FOF – 3-Year Return: 175%

#4 – HSBC Small Cap Fund – 3-Year Return: 167%

#5 – Tata Small Cap Fund – 3-Year Return: 160%

What are Beta and Alpha in Mutual Funds?
We have given beta and alpha metrics, hence providing detailed definition for investors to understand them. You can skip this section if you are already aware of them.
Beta – It is a measure of fund’s sensitivity to the market movement. Beta of less than 1 indicate that fund would have lower swing compared to ups and downs of the benchmark. Beta of more than 1 indicate that fund would have wider swings compared to benchmark. Investors should prefer lower beta funds which can have lesser swings compared to benchmark.
Alpha – It is a measure of extra returns provided by the fund compared to the benchmark. Investors should prefer high alpha funds which can generate higher returns. One should use Alpha and Beta together which goes hand in hand when comparing between risk and returns.

notes.title: Turmeric Ginger Tea
notes.text:
Turmeric Ginger Tea
Serving – 2

Preparation time – 10 minutes

Ingredients
Water: 3 cups (700 ml)
Fresh ginger, grated: 1 tbsp (30 g)
Whole cinnamon stick: 1
Grounded turmeric: 1 tsp (10 g)
Freshly squeezed lemon juice: ¼ cup (2 lemons)
Method
Boil the water in a medium-sized pan.
Add grated ginger, cinnamon stick, and ground turmeric and simmer for 5 minutes.
Take the pan off the heat and add lemon juice and stir well. Add honey if you wish to.
Strain and serve hot.

notes.title: Yoga Nidra’s Benefits Confirmed: Brain scans by scientists reveal how it relaxes the mind while awake
notes.text: https://m.economictimes.com/news/india/yoga-nidras-benefits-confirmed-brain-scans-by-scientists-reveal-how-it-relaxes-the-mind-while-awake/articleshow/113625271.cms

annotations.title: Yoga Nidra’s Benefits Confirmed: Brain scans by scientists reveal how it relaxes the mind while awake
annotations.description: Indian researchers have used functional MRI to study the effects of Yoga Nidra, revealing that experienced meditators exhibit unique neural mechanisms. The study found differences in the Default Mode Network, promoting deep relaxation while staying aware. This may explain its effectiveness in controlling anxiety and improving psychological well-being.
